negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Standard W&I policies exclude known issues, tax, and forward-looking warranties. Enhancement needed for full protection.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Enhanced W&I with: tax coverage uplift, synthetic warranty coverage, and de minimis/basket buy-down.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate enhancements: tax coverage, synthetic warranties for key risks, de minimis buy-down, and extended policy period.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Excessive conditions precedent giving buyer multiple walk-away opportunities
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Too many CPs create deal uncertainty and allow buyer to renegotiate or exit during CP satisfaction period.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: CPs limited to: regulatory approvals, material consents, and no MAC. Long-stop date for certainty.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit CPs to genuinely necessary items. Include: reasonable long-stop date, hell-or-high-water obligation for regulatory CPs.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Post-completion non-compete limited to 2-3 years in relevant sector and geography
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Reasonable non-compete protects goodwill acquired without unreasonably restricting seller.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 2-3 years, limited to business sold and geographic markets where business operates.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: scope matches actual business sold, geographic limitation is reasonable, and exceptions for passive investments.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Drag-along right without minimum price protection or equivalent terms for minority shareholders
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Drag-along without price floor allows majority to force minority sale at below fair value.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Drag-along with: minimum price (fair value), same terms as majority, and independent valuation if disputed.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: minimum price protection (independent valuation), tag-along parity, and consent threshold (75%+).

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Shareholders agreement with no deadlock resolution mechanism for 50:50 JV
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without deadlock resolution, 50:50 ventures can be paralysed by disagreement.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Tiered deadlock: CEO escalation, board mediation, then Russian roulette or Texas shoot-out.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include deadlock mechanism: escalation to CEOs, mediation, then put/call mechanism or independent valuation.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Good leaver definition extremely narrow, treating most departures as bad leaver
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Narrow good leaver definition means most departing employees lose equity value unfairly.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Good leaver: death, disability, redundancy, constructive dismissal, retirement. Bad: gross misconduct only.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Broaden good leaver to include: death, disability, redundancy, retirement, and termination without cause. Bad leaver for gross misconduct only.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Completion accounts without independent expert mechanism for disputes
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without dispute mechanism, completion accounts disagreements lead to expensive litigation.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Independent accountant (Big 4) as expert, decision binding, costs allocated by outcome.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include independent expert determination: Big 4 accountant, binding on parties, costs based on relative success.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service with no representation about training data provenance or licensing
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unknown training data provenance creates IP infringement risk for outputs used by customer.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Leading contracts require: training data provenance, licence compliance, and opt-out from customer data training.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require representation of lawful training data acquisition. Indemnity for IP claims arising from training data.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service provider disclaims all liability for inaccurate or hallucinated outputs
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Complete disclaimer for AI accuracy places all risk on customer regardless of use case.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Evolving. Provider accepts liability for outputs within marketed capabilities with reasonable accuracy targets.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require accuracy targets for specific use cases. Provider liable for outputs within marketed capabilities. Customer responsible for validation.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI provider can deprecate models without notice or migration assistance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Sudden model deprecation disrupts customer workflows and may break integrations.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Minimum 6-month deprecation notice with migration assistance and backward compatibility period.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: 6-month deprecation notice, 12-month backward compatibility, migration assistance, and documented differences.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI system making consequential decisions without explainability provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: EU AI Act requires explainability for high-risk AI systems. Absence creates compliance risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. High-risk AI: explainability documentation, audit trail, and human override capability.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: For high-risk use cases: require explainability documentation, decision audit trail, and human override mechanism.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service in regulated sector without bias monitoring or fairness testing obligations
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Biased AI outputs in regulated sectors create discrimination liability and regulatory risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Regular bias audits, fairness metrics, and corrective action commitments.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: regular bias audits (at least annual), defined fairness metrics, remediation SLA, and transparency reporting.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI system classified as high-risk under EU AI Act without human oversight mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: EU AI Act Article 14 requires human oversight for high-risk AI. Absence is non-compliant.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Human-in-the-loop or human-on-the-loop depending on risk level. Override capability mandatory.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable for high-risk AI. Include human oversight mechanism with: override capability, intervention logging, and escalation process.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service terms silent on IP ownership of customer-developed prompts and configurations
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Customer-developed prompts may represent significant IP investment. Ownership should be clear.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Customer typically owns prompts, configurations, and fine-tuning. Provider retains base model IP.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Clarify: customer owns all prompts, fine-tuning data, and configurations. Provider retains base model and platform IP.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No indemnity for AI-generated outputs that infringe third-party IP rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: AI outputs may inadvertently reproduce copyrighted material. Customer needs IP protection.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Leading providers offer limited IP indemnity for outputs generated through normal platform use.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require IP indemnity for AI outputs generated through normal platform use. Accept reasonable scope limitations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service with multi-tenant model where customer data may influence other tenants' outputs
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Cross-tenant data leakage through model training creates confidentiality and competitive risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Logical or physical tenant isolation for enterprise AI. No cross-tenant model influence.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require tenant isolation. Prohibit use of customer data to improve model for other tenants. Verify isolation architecture.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service contract includes comprehensive responsible AI framework with governance and auditing
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive responsible AI provisions demonstrate maturity and regulatory preparedness.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Leading practice. Includes: ethics board, bias testing, explainability, and incident response for AI failures.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: governance structure is independent, testing cadence is adequate, and incident response includes customer notification.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI provider claims ownership of synthetic data generated from customer source data
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Synthetic data derived from customer data should belong to customer or be jointly owned.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Customer typically owns synthetic data derived from their source data.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Customer owns synthetic data derived from customer source data. Provider may use anonymised aggregate insights only with consent.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI platform uses proprietary formats for models, configurations, and data with no export capability
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Proprietary formats without export create dependency and prevent migration to alternatives.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard export formats (ONNX, PMML). API-based data export. Configuration documentation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: standard model export formats, API-based data export, configuration documentation, and migration assistance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Supply chain contract without Scope 3 emissions reporting obligation despite CSRD applicability
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: CSRD requires value chain emissions reporting. Without supplier data, reporting obligations cannot be met.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual Scope 3 data reporting in GHG Protocol format. Emerging: science-based reduction targets.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require annual Scope 3 emissions data in GHG Protocol format. Include reduction targets and right to audit methodology.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International supply chain contract without modern slavery provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: UK Modern Slavery Act and similar legislation require supply chain due diligence and reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Anti-slavery clause with: due diligence obligation, audit rights, remediation process, and termination right.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include modern slavery clause: due diligence obligations, supply chain transparency, audit rights, and immediate termination for violations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: High-risk supply chain without human rights due diligence provisions per EU CSDDD
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive requires contractual cascading of due diligence obligations.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Contractual cascading of: identification, prevention, mitigation, and remediation of human rights impacts.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include CSDDD-aligned provisions: due diligence cascading, audit rights, remediation obligations, and termination for persistent violations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: ESG claims in contract not substantiated or potentially qualifying as greenwashing
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: EU Green Claims Directive will require substantiation. Unsubstantiated claims create liability.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. ESG claims backed by: third-party verification, specific metrics, and methodology disclosure.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require substantiation for all ESG claims: third-party verified data, methodology disclosure, and regular re-verification.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Contract includes comprehensive carbon disclosure with verified data and reduction targets
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive carbon disclosure meets regulatory requirements and enables value chain reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: GHG Protocol reporting, third-party verification, science-based targets, and annual improvement.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: verification body independence, target alignment with SBTi, reporting frequency, and data granularity.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Contract includes sustainability audit rights with CSRD-aligned reporting obligations
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Sustainability audit rights enable verification of ESG claims and regulatory compliance.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual sustainability audit right with 30-day notice and CSRD-aligned reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: audit scope covers environmental and social factors, cost allocation is fair, and remediation timelines defined.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No termination right for material ESG breaches by supplier
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: ESG breaches can create reputational, regulatory, and legal liability for buyer.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Right to terminate for material ESG breach after cure period (30-60 days for remediable breaches).
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include termination right for material ESG breach: immediate for child labour/forced labour, 60-day cure for other breaches.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Manufacturing supply chain without conflict minerals reporting or due diligence
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: EU Conflict Minerals Regulation and Dodd-Frank Section 1502 require supply chain due diligence.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains. RMI CMRT reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require conflict minerals due diligence: CMRT reporting, smelter identification, and OECD-aligned due diligence process.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract without mechanism to protect commercially sensitive information from FOI
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: FOI legislation applies to government contracts. Without protection, trade secrets may be disclosed.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: FOI clause with: obligation to consult before disclosure, ability to redact commercial information, and exemption process.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include FOI clause: consultation obligation before disclosure, right to request exemptions, and clear marking of confidential information.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government step-in rights exercisable for any performance concern without defined threshold
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Broad step-in rights create uncertainty and potential for overreach by contracting authority.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Step-in triggered by: material and persistent SLA failure, emergency, or insolvency only.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit step-in triggers to: material persistent failure (defined), emergency affecting public services, and insolvency events.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Social value obligations imposed without corresponding funding or pricing recognition
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unfunded social value obligations reduce contract profitability and may be unsustainable.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Social value costed as part of overall contract value or separately funded.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept social value obligations but ensure: costed within contract price, measurable KPIs, and proportionate to contract value.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Security clearance requirements not specified or timeline for obtaining clearance unrealistic
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Clearance processing can take 3-12 months. Unrealistic timelines cause delivery delays.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Clearance requirements specified upfront. 3-6 month lead time. Interim measures for period pending clearance.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Specify clearance levels required. Allow realistic timeline (6 months minimum). Define interim arrangements pending clearance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract includes break clause at defined intervals with reasonable notice and compensation
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Break clauses at regular intervals are standard in government contracts and provide necessary flexibility.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual break option with 6-month notice and compensation for demobilisation costs.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: notice period is adequate (minimum 6 months), compensation covers demobilisation, and transition assistance included.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract with mandatory benchmarking and automatic price adjustment
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Automatic downward price adjustment without upward adjustment creates asymmetric risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Benchmarking with both upward and downward adjustment, or downward only with floor protection.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept benchmarking but require: bilateral adjustment, methodology agreed upfront, and floor at cost-plus-margin minimum.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract with clear data sovereignty requirements: domestic processing, storage, and personnel
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Clear data sovereignty requirements eliminate ambiguity about data location and access.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Government data: domestic processing and storage. Personnel: cleared nationals. Subcontractors: pre-approved.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: infrastructure meets requirements, personnel have required clearance, and subcontractor approval process defined.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Most favoured customer clause requiring lowest price across all customers globally
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Global MFC is commercially unreasonable as pricing varies by market, volume, and scope.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: MFC limited to: same jurisdiction, comparable scope, and comparable volume band.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit MFC to: comparable scope and volume in same jurisdiction. Exclude: volume discounts, bundled deals, and legacy contracts.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Large outsourcing without open book accounting or cost transparency mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without cost transparency, pricing fairness is unverifiable over long contract terms.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Open book accounting for cost-plus contracts. Transparency reporting for fixed-price above EUR 1M/year.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require open book or cost transparency reporting for contracts above EUR 500K/year. Include audit rights over cost basis.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Long-term outsourcing without gain-sharing mechanism for efficiency improvements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without gain-sharing, all efficiency benefits accrue to provider, misaligning incentives.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 50:50 gain-share on agreed baseline improvements. Reviewed annually.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include gain-sharing mechanism: 50:50 share of savings against agreed baseline. Annual review and rebaseline.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Contract includes irrevocable letter of credit from reputable bank with clear draw-down conditions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: LC provides payment security for high-value contracts and international transactions.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Irrevocable, confirmed LC from rated bank. Draw-down on presentation of compliant documents.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: issuing bank rating, confirmation requirement, draw-down conditions are objective, and expiry aligns with payment schedule.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Performance bond requirement exceeds 20% of contract value for services contract
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Excessive bond requirements increase costs disproportionately for service contracts.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Performance bond: 10% of contract value for construction, 5-10% for services. Parent company guarantee as alternative.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Reduce bond to 10% of contract value. Accept parent company guarantee as alternative. Phase out after first year of satisfactory performance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No late payment interest provision despite statutory entitlement
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Late Payment of Commercial Debts Act (UK) and similar EU legislation provides statutory interest right.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Contractual interest at statutory rate or 2-4% above base rate. Compound monthly.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include late payment interest at statutory rate or 3% above base rate. Reference applicable Late Payment legislation.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Contract with subsidiary company without parent guarantee for material obligations
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Subsidiary may lack financial substance to meet obligations. Parent guarantee provides backstop.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Parent guarantee for subsidiaries with insufficient standalone financial strength. Commonly required above EUR 1M/year.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require parent company guarantee for all material obligations. Alternative: performance bond or escrow.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Escrow arrangement with independent escrow agent, clear release conditions, and regular verification
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Well-structured escrow protects both parties in high-value or high-risk transactions.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Independent escrow agent. Release conditions objective and documented. Annual verification of escrow contents.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: escrow agent independence, release conditions are objective, verification schedule, and dispute resolution for escrow disputes.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Outsourcing contract without stranded costs provision for early termination
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Provider may have made investments (infrastructure, hiring) that are irrecoverable on early termination.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Stranded costs schedule: documented investments, depreciation schedule, and declining recovery over term.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include stranded costs schedule: documented upfront, depreciated over contract term, capped at actual investment.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: IP licence with ambiguous scope: unclear territory, field of use, or sublicensing rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Ambiguous licence scope leads to disputes about permitted use and sublicensing.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Clear specification of: territory, field of use, exclusivity, sublicensing rights, and duration.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define precisely: territory (named countries or worldwide), field of use, exclusivity, sublicensing (if any), and duration.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Blanket non-assertion covenant covering all present and future IP against all uses
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Overbroad non-assertion effectively transfers IP rights without compensation.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Non-assertion limited to: specific patent families, specific products, and specific field of use.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit non-assertion to: identified patents, specific products/services, and defined field of use. Preserve rights for other uses.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: IP licence without warranty that licensor has right to grant licence
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without title warranty, licensee has no recourse if licensor lacked rights to license.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Warranty of title and right to licence is fundamental. Combined with IP infringement indemnity.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require warranty that licensor has full right to grant the licence and it does not infringe third-party rights.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Clear background IP reservation with scheduled identification and appropriate licence grants
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Clear background IP identification prevents disputes about ownership and use rights.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Background IP scheduled, reserved to owner, with necessary licences granted for project use.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: background IP schedule is complete, licences are sufficient for project needs, and improvement ownership is clear.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA lacks processor obligation to cooperate with controller DPIAs
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: GDPR Article 28(3)(f) requires processor cooperation with DPIAs. Absence is non-compliant.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Processor must assist controller with DPIAs and prior consultations at reasonable cost.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include DPIA cooperation obligation. Reasonable assistance at agreed rates. Timely response to DPIA requests.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA does not require processor assistance with data subject rights requests
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: GDPR Article 28(3)(e) mandates processor assistance with data subject requests.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Processor provides technical measures and assistance for responding to data subject requests within 5 business days.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Include data subject rights assistance with: 5 business day response time, technical measures, and reasonable cost basis.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA with vague or broad processing scope that exceeds actual service requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Broad processing scope violates data minimisation principle and increases risk exposure.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Processing scope precisely defined: subject matter, duration, nature, purpose, data categories, and data subjects.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define processing scope precisely in Annex: subject matter, duration, nature, purpose, personal data categories, and data subject categories.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA references 'appropriate technical and organisational measures' without specifying them
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Vague security measures make compliance verification impossible and create audit disputes.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Detailed TOMs annex: encryption standards, access controls, monitoring, incident response, and testing.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require detailed TOMs annex specifying: encryption (AES-256), access controls, monitoring, incident response, and annual pen testing.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Cross-border data transfer without Transfer Impact Assessment
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Post-Schrems II, TIA is mandatory for all cross-border transfers to assess adequacy of protection.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Documented TIA covering: legal framework assessment, supplementary measures, and regular review.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require completed TIA before any cross-border transfer. Include: legal framework assessment, supplementary measures, and annual review.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: High-risk processing activity without requirement for Data Protection Impact Assessment
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: GDPR Article 35 requires DPIA for processing likely to result in high risk. Absence is non-compliant.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: DPIA mandatory for: large-scale profiling, automated decision-making, sensitive data processing.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require DPIA for all high-risk processing. Share DPIA results with data subjects where appropriate.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Data localisation requirements clearly defined with specific data centre locations and failover provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Clear data localisation eliminates ambiguity about data location and regulatory compliance.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Named data centre locations, failover within same jurisdiction, and notification of any location change.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: failover locations meet same requirements, disaster recovery site within jurisdiction, and no CDN caching outside scope.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Consulting engagement with open-ended scope and time-and-materials billing without cap
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Uncapped T&M with undefined scope creates unlimited cost exposure for client.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Defined scope with T&M estimate and cap. Change orders for additional scope.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define scope with estimated hours and cap at 110-120% of estimate. Require written approval for overruns. Change orders for new scope.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Consulting deliverables warranted only to 'commercially reasonable' standard
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Commercially reasonable is vague and lower than professional standards expected of consultants.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Professional standard of care consistent with industry best practice and relevant qualifications.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: services performed with reasonable skill and care consistent with best industry practice by appropriately qualified personnel.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Consulting engagement without conflict of interest representation or ongoing disclosure obligation
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Undisclosed conflicts can compromise advice quality and create liability.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Representation of no current conflicts with ongoing obligation to disclose and manage conflicts.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: representation of no current conflicts, ongoing disclosure obligation, and right to terminate if material conflict arises.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Consulting engagement with clear SOW, defined deliverables, and formal change control process
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Well-defined scope with change control prevents scope creep and cost overruns.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Detailed SOW, acceptance criteria, change order process, and impact assessment for changes.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: deliverable definitions are objective, acceptance criteria measurable, and change order process has timeline and cost impact assessment.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International trade contract using inappropriate Incoterms for the transaction type
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Wrong Incoterms create misalignment of risk, cost, and insurance responsibilities.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Incoterms 2020 selected based on: mode of transport, risk appetite, and insurance capabilities.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Review Incoterms selection against: transport mode, risk allocation preference, and insurance arrangements. CIF/CIP for sea/multimodal.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International contract without sanctions screening obligation for counterparty and transactions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Sanctions violations carry severe penalties including criminal liability and asset freezing.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Ongoing sanctions screening of counterparties, beneficial owners, and transactions against OFAC/EU/UN lists.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Include sanctions compliance: screening obligations, ongoing monitoring, notification duties, and termination for breach.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Technology transfer contract without export control compliance provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Export control violations carry severe penalties. Dual-use technology requires specific licences.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Export control clause: classification responsibility, licence obligations, end-use certificates, and re-export restrictions.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include export control clause: ECCN classification, licence obligations, end-user certificate requirements, and re-export prohibition.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Multi-year international contract in foreign currency without hedging or adjustment mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Currency fluctuations can significantly impact contract economics over multi-year terms.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Currency clause: adjustment mechanism, hedging obligations, or pricing in stable currency.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include currency risk allocation: adjustment corridor (+/-5%), periodic repricing, or denomination in stable currency.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International contract with comprehensive anti-bribery provisions aligned with UKBA and FCPA
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive anti-bribery provisions protect both parties from extraterritorial enforcement risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Anti-bribery clause: compliance representation, adequate procedures, audit rights, and termination for breach.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: compliance programme details, training obligations, reporting channels, and investigation cooperation.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International contract with ICC arbitration in neutral venue with defined procedural rules
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: ICC arbitration provides neutral, enforceable dispute resolution for cross-border contracts.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: ICC arbitration in neutral venue (Paris, London, Singapore, Zurich). 1 or 3 arbitrators depending on value.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: seat of arbitration, language, number of arbitrators appropriate to value, and emergency arbitrator provisions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Long-term international contract without hardship clause for fundamental change in circumstances
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without hardship clause, parties are bound even when circumstances make performance fundamentally different.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Hardship clause per ICC/UNIDROIT: renegotiation obligation, defined triggers, and termination if no agreement.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include hardship clause: defined triggers (currency >20%, regulatory change, sanctions), renegotiation period, and termination as last resort.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Healthcare vendor handling PHI without executed Business Associate Agreement
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: HIPAA requires BAA for any business associate handling PHI. Absence is a direct violation.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: BAA mandatory per 45 CFR 164.502(e). Must include all required provisions of 164.504(e).
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Execute BAA covering all 45 CFR 164.504(e) requirements before any PHI access.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: BAA allows more than 30 days for breach notification to covered entity
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: HIPAA requires notification without unreasonable delay and within 60 days maximum. Covered entity needs time to assess.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Business associate notification within 5-10 business days to allow covered entity time for 60-day reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require breach notification within 5 business days. Include: nature of breach, PHI involved, mitigation steps, and corrective actions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: BAA permits use of PHI beyond minimum necessary for contracted services
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: HIPAA minimum necessary principle requires limiting PHI use to what is needed for the service.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Use limited to: contracted services, legal obligations, and proper management with de-identification for analytics.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit permitted uses to minimum necessary for contracted services. Require de-identification per HIPAA Safe Harbor for any analytics.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: BAA includes comprehensive administrative, physical, and technical safeguards aligned with NIST 800-66
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive HIPAA safeguards meet regulatory requirements and demonstrate due diligence.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: All 45 CFR 164 Subpart C safeguards with NIST 800-66 alignment and annual risk assessment.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: annual risk assessment commitment, encryption at rest and in transit, access controls, and audit logging.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SaaS agreement without data portability or export capability
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without data portability, customer is effectively locked into the platform.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Data export in standard formats (CSV, JSON, XML) via API and bulk download.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: data export in standard formats (CSV/JSON/XML), API-based export, bulk download capability, and 90-day post-termination access.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SaaS provider can deprecate APIs without notice or migration period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: API deprecation without notice breaks customer integrations and workflows.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 12-month API deprecation notice with backward compatibility and migration assistance.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: 12-month deprecation notice, 6-month backward compatibility, migration documentation, and technical assistance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Usage-based SaaS pricing without customer-accessible metering or audit capability
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Opaque metering means customer cannot verify charges. Potential for billing disputes.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Real-time usage dashboard, downloadable usage reports, and right to audit metering.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: real-time usage dashboard, downloadable usage data, metering methodology documentation, and right to audit.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SaaS with 99.9% uptime, tiered credits, exclusions defined, and termination right for persistent failure
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive SaaS SLA with meaningful financial incentives and exit right.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 99.9% uptime is standard for enterprise SaaS with 10-30% credit tiers.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: measurement methodology, maintenance window exclusions are reasonable, and credit application is automatic.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Multi-tenant SaaS without documented tenant isolation and security architecture
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without documented isolation, cross-tenant data leakage risk is unassessed.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Documented tenant isolation: logical separation, encryption per tenant, and access controls.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require documentation of: tenant isolation architecture, encryption per tenant, access control model, and annual pen test results.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Large volume procurement without volume discount tiers or commitment pricing
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without volume incentives, buyer loses leverage that comes with scale.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Volume discount tiers: 10% for 2x volume, 15% for 3x, 20% for 5x. Annual true-up.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate volume discount tiers based on annual spend. Include annual true-up and retroactive adjustment.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Sole-source agreement with no termination for convenience and no alternative sourcing rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Sole-source lock-in without exit creates total dependency on single supplier.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Even sole-source contracts include termination for convenience (with reasonable notice) and technology escrow.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: termination for convenience (180-day notice), technology escrow, and right to qualify alternative sources after year 2.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Multi-year contract with price lock for initial term and CPI-capped increases for renewals
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Price lock with capped increases provides budget certainty while allowing fair adjustments.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Price lock for 12-24 months. Increases capped at CPI or 3-5% per annum thereafter.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: price lock duration, CPI index reference, cap percentage, and application date.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Service KPIs defined but no financial consequence for underperformance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: KPIs without consequences are aspirational targets with no enforcement mechanism.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: KPIs linked to: service credits (financial), remediation plans (operational), and termination (ultimate).
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Link KPIs to: tiered service credits for underperformance, mandatory remediation plans, and termination right for persistent failure.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DORA-regulated ICT contract with vague or high-level service description
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: DORA Article 30(2)(a) requires clear and complete description of ICT services.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Detailed service description schedule: functions, data processing, service levels, and dependencies.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable for DORA compliance. Include detailed service description: functions, data flows, dependencies, and criticality assessment.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Critical ICT contract without DORA-compliant exit strategy
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: DORA Article 28(8) requires exit strategies for critical ICT third-party services.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Exit strategy: transition plan, data migration, knowledge transfer, and alternative provider qualification.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Include exit strategy: transition plan with timelines, data migration, knowledge transfer, and no degradation during transition.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DORA-regulated contract allowing subcontracting of critical functions without financial entity oversight
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: DORA Article 30(2)(a)(vi) requires oversight of subcontracting chains for critical functions.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Prior approval for subcontracting critical functions. Sub-contractor flow-down of all DORA obligations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: prior approval for subcontracting, flow-down of DORA obligations, right to object, and sub-contractor audit rights.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DORA-compliant incident reporting with classification framework and defined timelines
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive incident reporting meeting DORA requirements for ICT-related incidents.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Major ICT incident notification within 4 hours. Classification per DORA taxonomy. Root cause analysis within 30 days.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: classification aligns with DORA taxonomy, notification timeline meets regulatory requirement, and RCA process defined.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Critical ICT contract without threat-led penetration testing provisions per DORA
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: DORA Article 26 requires threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) for critical ICT services.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual TLPT with results sharing, remediation obligations, and regulatory authority access.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include TLPT provisions: annual testing, results shared with financial entity, remediation SLA, and regulatory access to results.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: NIS2-regulated supply chain contract with incident notification exceeding 24 hours
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: NIS2 Article 23 requires early warning within 24 hours and full notification within 72 hours.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Supplier notification within 12 hours to allow entity time for 24-hour early warning.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: 12-hour initial notification, 48-hour detailed notification, and 30-day final report with root cause analysis.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: NIS2-regulated contract without defined patching SLAs for critical vulnerabilities
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: NIS2 Article 21(2)(e) requires vulnerability handling and disclosure. Patching SLAs are essential.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Critical: 24 hours. High: 7 days. Medium: 30 days. Low: 90 days. Zero-day: immediate mitigation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define patching SLAs: critical 24h, high 7d, medium 30d, low 90d. Zero-day: immediate mitigation with patch within 48h.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Supply chain contract with full NIS2 Article 21(2)(d) supply chain security requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive supply chain security provisions meeting NIS2 requirements.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Supplier security assessment, ongoing monitoring, flow-down of requirements, and incident response coordination.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: sub-supplier assessment process, monitoring frequency, flow-down completeness, and incident coordination procedures.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: NIS2-regulated contract without access control requirements for supplier personnel
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: NIS2 Article 21(2)(i) requires access control policies. Supplier access must be governed.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Least privilege access, MFA, regular access reviews, and immediate revocation on personnel change.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: least privilege access, MFA for all access, quarterly access reviews, and immediate revocation on personnel departure.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liability regime without de minimis threshold filtering minor claims
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without liability floor, administrative burden of processing small claims is disproportionate.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: De minimis: EUR 5,000-25,000 depending on contract value. Basket: 0.5-1% of cap.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include de minimis threshold (EUR 10,000) below which claims are excluded. Add aggregate basket before cap applies.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Excessive unlimited liability carve-outs that effectively negate the liability cap
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Too many carve-outs make the cap meaningless, exposing party to effectively unlimited liability.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Unlimited carve-outs limited to: death/personal injury, fraud, and wilful misconduct only.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit unlimited carve-outs to: death/personal injury, fraud, and wilful misconduct. All other carve-outs subject to super-cap.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Indemnification for regulatory fines without considering whether fines are legally indemnifiable
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: In many jurisdictions, indemnification for regulatory fines may be unenforceable as contrary to public policy.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Indemnify for fines where legally permissible. Separate indemnity for: investigation costs, remediation, and notification.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Indemnify for fines 'to the extent legally permissible.' Ensure separate coverage for: investigation costs, remediation, and notification costs.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Confidentiality clause treats trade secrets same as general confidential information
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Trade secrets require indefinite protection, not time-limited confidentiality.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Trade secrets: indefinite protection. General CI: 3-5 years. Definition of trade secret aligned with Trade Secrets Directive.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define trade secrets separately with indefinite protection. General CI: 5-year survival. Align with EU Trade Secrets Directive.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Confidentiality exceptions include legal compulsion but no notification or minimisation process
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without process, compelled disclosure may be broader than legally required.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Legal compulsion exception with: advance notification (where permitted), minimisation of disclosure, and legal challenge assistance.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include process for compelled disclosure: advance notification where legally permitted, disclosure minimisation, and reasonable assistance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Termination clause triggered by counterparty insolvency without considering ipso facto restrictions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Many jurisdictions restrict ipso facto clauses. Automatic termination on insolvency may be unenforceable.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Right to terminate on insolvency but subject to applicable insolvency law restrictions. Include: administration moratorium.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include insolvency termination right subject to applicable insolvency law. Provide for: service continuity, data protection, and priority claims.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Change of control provision with notification, assessment period, and termination right if unacceptable
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Well-structured change of control protects against unwanted changes in contract counterparty.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 30-day notification, 90-day assessment, termination without penalty if new controller unacceptable.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: change of control definition covers direct and indirect changes, assessment period is adequate, and no penalty on exit.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Arbitration clause without carve-out for urgent interim or injunctive relief from courts
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Arbitration timelines may be too slow for urgent matters like IP infringement or data breach.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Arbitration as primary with court carve-out for: interim relief, injunctions, and emergency measures.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include carve-out: either party may seek interim/injunctive relief from courts without waiving arbitration. Emergency arbitrator as supplement.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Multi-tier dispute resolution with defined escalation, timeframes, and preservation of rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Multi-tier process encourages settlement while preserving formal dispute resolution rights.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Tier 1: operational managers (15 days). Tier 2: senior executives (30 days). Tier 3: mediation/arbitration.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: timeframes are realistic, escalation contacts defined, and each tier preserves limitation period rights.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Force majeure clause without obligation to mitigate impact of FM event
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without mitigation duty, FM can be used as excuse for complete inaction.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: FM with: reasonable endeavours to mitigate, regular progress updates, and alternative performance measures.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include mitigation duty: reasonable endeavours to overcome FM, regular progress reports, and implementation of workarounds.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Force majeure excludes supply chain disruptions despite global supply chain volatility
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Post-COVID and geopolitical disruptions make supply chain FM exclusion potentially unfair.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Evolving. Supply chain disruptions partially covered: only if beyond reasonable mitigation through diversification.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include supply chain disruption as FM only where: beyond reasonable mitigation, diversification implemented, and alternative sourcing attempted.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Contract with multiple governing laws for different sections creating complexity
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Split governing laws create interpretation challenges and increased legal costs.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Single governing law for entire agreement. Exceptions only where legally required (e.g., local employment law).
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Consolidate to single governing law where possible. If split necessary, clearly delineate which law governs which provisions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Software or IP deliverable without non-infringement warranty
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without non-infringement warranty, customer bears all risk of third-party IP claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Provider warrants deliverables do not infringe third-party IP. Combined with IP indemnification.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require non-infringement warranty backed by IP indemnification. Include: replace/modify if infringing, and refund as last resort.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual warranty of compliance with all applicable laws including data protection and anti-bribery
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive compliance warranty provides contractual backstop for regulatory obligations.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Mutual compliance warranty covering: data protection, anti-bribery, export controls, and sector regulations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: scope covers all relevant regulatory areas, ongoing obligation (not just at signing), and notification for non-compliance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Milestone payments without objective acceptance criteria for each milestone
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Subjective milestones lead to payment disputes and project delays.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Each milestone with: objective acceptance criteria, testing period, and sign-off process.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define objective acceptance criteria for each milestone. Include: 10-day acceptance period, right to reject with specifics, and cure period.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Unrestricted set-off right allowing one party to withhold payment against any claimed amounts
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unrestricted set-off can be used to withhold legitimate payments for disputed claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Set-off limited to: undisputed amounts, amounts determined by court/arbitration, and admitted claims.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit set-off to undisputed amounts or amounts determined by binding dispute resolution. No set-off for disputed amounts.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Assignment to affiliates permitted without requiring affiliate to meet same standards
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unrestricted affiliate assignment may transfer obligations to entities unable to perform.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Affiliate assignment with: same or better financial standing, capability requirement, and assignor remains guarantor.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Allow affiliate assignment but require: equivalent financial standing, capability to perform, and assignor remains liable as guarantor.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SLA defines response time targets but no resolution time targets
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Response without resolution means acknowledgement occurs but fix may take indefinitely.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Both response AND resolution targets by severity: P1 response 15min/resolve 4hr, P2 1hr/8hr, P3 4hr/5days.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Add resolution targets: P1 4-hour resolution, P2 8-hour, P3 5 business days, P4 20 business days. Escalation at 50% of target.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SLA exclusions so broad they effectively negate the uptime commitment
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Excessive exclusions (customer actions, third-party services, planned maintenance, force majeure) can reduce real uptime to below 99%.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Exclusions limited to: customer-caused issues, pre-approved maintenance windows, and true force majeure.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit exclusions to: customer-caused issues, pre-approved maintenance (max 8hr/month), and genuine force majeure. All other downtime counts.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No representation of accuracy of information provided during negotiations or due diligence
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without accuracy representation, misleading pre-contractual information has limited remedy.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Representation that all information provided is true, accurate, complete, and not misleading.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include representation of accuracy for all information provided. Combine with entire agreement fraud carve-out.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Technology contract without export control compliance provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Export control violations carry criminal penalties and debarment from government contracts.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Export control clause: classification, licence obligations, end-use restrictions, and deemed export provisions.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable for technology. Include: classification responsibility, licence obligations, end-use certificates, and re-export restrictions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Financial services contract without AML/KYC compliance provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: AML regulations require contractual provisions for customer due diligence and transaction monitoring.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: AML clause: KYC obligations, transaction monitoring, suspicious activity reporting, and record-keeping.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include AML provisions: KYC verification, ongoing monitoring, suspicious activity reporting, and record retention per applicable regulations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Data-handling service provider without cyber insurance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without cyber insurance, provider may lack financial resources to respond to data breach.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Cyber insurance minimum EUR 2-5M per occurrence covering: breach response, notification, regulatory fines, and business interruption.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require cyber insurance minimum EUR 2M. Coverage: incident response, notification costs, regulatory fines (where insurable), and business interruption.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Professional indemnity insurance below contract value or industry minimums
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Inadequate PI insurance means insufficient financial backing for professional negligence claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: PI insurance: minimum of contract value or EUR 2M (whichever higher). Maintained for 6 years post-completion.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require PI insurance at minimum of annual contract value or EUR 2M. Maintained for 6 years post-completion. Certificate provided annually.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Audit rights include regulatory authority access provisions meeting DORA/NIS2 requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Regulatory audit access is mandatory under DORA and NIS2 for regulated entities.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Direct regulatory authority access to provider premises, data, and personnel upon request.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: no pre-conditions on regulatory access, documentation readily available, and cooperation obligation defined.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Audit costs always borne by auditing party regardless of findings
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: If audit reveals material issues, provider should bear costs as they caused the need for deeper investigation.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Auditing party bears costs unless material non-compliance found, then provider bears costs.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Cost allocation based on findings: auditing party bears costs if no issues; provider bears costs if material non-compliance discovered.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Exclusive distribution agreement without minimum purchase commitment or performance targets
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Exclusivity without minimums means distributor can block market access without performing.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Exclusive distribution with: annual minimum purchase, quarterly targets, and conversion to non-exclusive for underperformance.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include minimum annual purchase commitment. Automatic conversion to non-exclusive if minimums not met for 2 consecutive quarters.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Distribution territories not clearly defined or overlapping with other distributors
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Territory overlaps create channel conflict and customer confusion.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Clearly defined territories by: country, region, or customer segment. No overlap between distributors.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define territories precisely. Include: named countries/regions, customer segment allocation, and conflict resolution for edge cases.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Managed services contract without defined governance structure and review cadence
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without governance, service issues escalate without structured resolution path.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Monthly operational review, quarterly strategic review, annual contract review. Named representatives.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include governance schedule: monthly operational review, quarterly strategic review, annual contract review with named representatives.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Managed services engagement without transition-in plan and knowledge transfer period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without transition-in, service quality suffers during handover from incumbent.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 60-90 day transition-in period with: knowledge transfer, shadow running, and acceptance criteria.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require transition-in plan: 90-day period, knowledge transfer from incumbent, shadow operations, and defined go-live criteria.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Deliverables contain copyleft-licensed components without disclosure or customer approval
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Copyleft obligations may force disclosure of proprietary source code.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: SBOM delivery, no copyleft without written approval, approved licence list, and IP indemnity for OSS.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: pre-approved licence list (no copyleft without approval), SBOM in SPDX format, and OSS IP indemnification.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Open source policy with approved licence list, SBOM delivery, and compliance monitoring
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive OSS governance prevents licence contamination and ensures compliance.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Approved licence list, SBOM in SPDX/CycloneDX, automated scanning, and remediation process.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: licence list is appropriate, SBOM format is standard, scanning is automated, and remediation SLA defined.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Joint venture without reserved matters requiring unanimous or supermajority consent
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without reserved matters, majority partner can unilaterally make material decisions.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Reserved matters covering: budget changes >10%, new debt, related party transactions, and constitutional changes.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include comprehensive reserved matters: budget changes, new debt/security, related party transactions, litigation, and constitutional changes.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Minority shareholder without adequate information and inspection rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without information rights, minority shareholder cannot monitor their investment.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Monthly management accounts, annual audited accounts, budget approval, and access to books and records.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: monthly management accounts, quarterly board meetings, annual audited accounts, budget approval, and inspection rights.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: JV/shareholder agreement without tag-along rights for minority shareholders
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without tag-along, majority can sell to third party leaving minority with unwanted partner.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Tag-along on same terms as majority sale. Triggered by sale of shares above defined threshold.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include tag-along rights: triggered by sale above 50% threshold, on same price and terms as majority sale.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Standard Contractual Clauses using incorrect module for the processing relationship
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Using wrong SCC module means transfer mechanism is invalid and transfer is unlawful.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Module 1: C2C. Module 2: C2P. Module 3: P2P. Module 4: P2C. Select based on actual relationship.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Verify module selection matches actual relationship: C2P for most SaaS (Module 2), P2P for sub-processing (Module 3).

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SCCs executed without supplementary measures despite EDPB Recommendations 01/2020
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Post-Schrems II, SCCs alone may be insufficient. Supplementary measures often required.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Supplementary measures: encryption in transit and at rest, pseudonymisation, access controls, and transparency reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Implement supplementary measures per EDPB Recommendations: encryption, pseudonymisation, access restrictions, and transparency reports.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual non-solicitation limited to 12 months covering directly involved personnel only
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Balanced mutual non-solicitation protects both parties' workforce investment.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 12-month mutual non-solicitation limited to individuals directly involved in the engagement.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: applies to active solicitation only (not responses to general recruitment), mutual, and scope is proportionate.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Customer requires additional insured status on provider's insurance policy
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Additional insured status provides direct access to insurance coverage for third-party claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Additional insured for general liability. Usually not available for PI or cyber insurance.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Provide additional insured status on general liability policy. For PI/cyber: provide certificate of insurance and notification of material changes.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Clear exclusion of third-party beneficiary rights with appropriate carve-outs
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Excluding third-party rights prevents unintended enforcement while preserving intentional beneficiaries.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard exclusion with carve-outs for: indemnified parties, affiliates (where intended), and permitted assignees.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: carve-outs cover all intentional third-party beneficiaries and permitted assignees.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Usage-based pricing without any cost cap or spend alert mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Uncapped usage pricing creates risk of unexpectedly high bills from usage spikes or errors.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Usage cap or spend alert at defined thresholds. Pre-agreed overage rate. Monthly cap option.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: spend alerts at 80%/100% of expected usage, monthly cap option, pre-agreed overage rate, and right to dispute anomalous charges.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Recurring payment contract with automatic annual increase without notification
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Silent automatic increases prevent budget planning and remove customer negotiation opportunity.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 90-day advance notice of any price increase. Opt-out right within 30 days of notification.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require 90-day advance written notice of any price increase. Include opt-out right and cap increases at CPI or 5%.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Secondment arrangement without clear allocation of employer duties of care
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unclear duty of care allocation can leave secondee without adequate protection or recourse.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Host organisation assumes day-to-day duty of care. Home employer retains employment obligations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Clearly allocate: host has day-to-day duty of care and H&S obligations. Home employer retains employment terms and insurance coverage.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Data sharing agreement without specified purposes or purpose limitation principle
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without purpose limitation, shared data may be used for unintended purposes violating GDPR Article 5(1)(b).
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes. No further processing incompatible with original purposes.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define specific purposes for data sharing. Include: purpose limitation, data minimisation, and prohibition on incompatible further processing.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Maintenance contract without end-of-life notification or extended support provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without EOL provisions, customer may face sudden loss of support for critical systems.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 24-month EOL notification. Extended support available at premium rate. Migration assistance.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: 24-month advance EOL notification, extended support option (up to 36 months), and migration assistance to successor product.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: OEM agreement without quality control standards or right to inspect
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without quality control, OEM products may not meet required standards, damaging brand.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Quality standards specification, incoming inspection rights, rejection process, and continuous improvement.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: quality standards specification, incoming inspection right, batch rejection process, and quarterly quality reviews.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Long-term strategic partnership with perpetual exclusivity and no periodic review
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Perpetual exclusivity without review may become commercially unreasonable over time.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Exclusivity reviewed every 2-3 years based on performance criteria. Conversion to non-exclusive for underperformance.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include exclusivity review every 2 years. Conversion to non-exclusive if performance targets not met for 2 consecutive periods.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Intern or contractor agreement without IP assignment for work product
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without assignment, IP in work product may belong to intern/contractor by default.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Work product IP assignment to company. Moral rights waiver where applicable. Background IP reserved.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include IP assignment for all work product. Confirm: present assignment of future IP, moral rights waiver, and background IP reservation.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: M&A NDA without standstill or non-solicitation provision
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without standstill, recipient of confidential information may make hostile approach.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 12-18 month standstill on public approaches. Non-solicitation of key employees.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: 12-month standstill on hostile approaches, non-solicitation of key employees, and no-hire provision.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Consortium agreement with joint and several liability between members
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Joint and several liability means each member is liable for entire consortium obligation.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Several liability proportionate to scope of work. Joint and several only where client requires.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate several liability based on proportion of scope. If client requires joint-and-several, include: internal indemnities and cap per member.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Senior executive contract without enhanced redundancy provisions above statutory minimum
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Statutory redundancy is often minimal. Senior roles typically include enhanced packages.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Enhanced redundancy: 1-3 months per year of service, capped at 12-24 months.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate enhanced redundancy: 1 month per year of service (minimum), capped at 12 months, plus outplacement support.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Social media policy restricts all personal social media activity including outside work hours
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Overbroad social media restrictions may violate freedom of expression and privacy rights.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Policy limited to: not disclosing confidential information, not bringing employer into disrepute, and clear work/personal distinction.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit social media policy to: confidentiality protection, no employer disrepute, and clear disclaimer requirements for personal opinions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Training repayment clause requiring full repayment regardless of when employee leaves or reason for departure
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Full repayment without pro-rata reduction or exception for involuntary departure is excessive.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Pro-rata reducing balance over 12-24 months. Waived for: redundancy, constructive dismissal, and employer breach.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept pro-rata repayment reducing monthly over 24 months. Waive for: redundancy, constructive dismissal, and material employer breach.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Employment contract with contractual hybrid/flexible working rights and clear framework
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Contractual flexible working provides certainty and reflects modern working practices.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Hybrid working: 2-3 days office minimum. Contractual right with reasonable variation mechanism.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: core hours defined, equipment provisions, health and safety for home workers, and data security requirements.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Blanket prohibition on all outside activities without exception for non-competing roles
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Total prohibition of outside work may be unenforceable and overly restrictive.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Disclosure and approval for outside activities. Automatic approval for non-competing, non-conflicting work.
