negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept disclosure obligation. Allow non-competing outside activities with notification. Require approval only for potentially conflicting roles.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Sales commission with broad clawback for customer churn regardless of reason
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Clawback for churn beyond salesperson control is unfair and demotivating.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Clawback limited to: first 3-6 months, customer cancellation only (not downsell), and capped at commission paid.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit clawback to: 3-month window, customer-initiated cancellations only, and maximum 50% of commission paid. No clawback for downsells.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Employment contract prohibits providing substantive references to future employers
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Restriction to name/dates only references hinders employee career progression.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Factual reference including: role, dates, and confirmation of good standing. No prohibition on substantive references.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include entitlement to factual reference including: role, dates, responsibilities, and absence of disciplinary concerns.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Multi-phase construction project without sectional completion mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without sectional completion, delay damages cannot be applied per section and partial handover is complicated.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Sectional completion with: section-specific dates, separate delay damages, and partial possession provisions.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include sectional completion schedule: section dates, section-specific delay damages, and clear partial possession mechanism.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Construction project without clear allocation of CDM Regulations duties
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: CDM Regulations impose mandatory duties. Failure to allocate creates criminal liability risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Clear allocation of: Client, Principal Designer, Principal Contractor, and Designer duties per CDM 2015.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Allocate all CDM duties explicitly: Client duties, PD appointment, PC appointment, and welfare facilities.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Construction contract includes Building Safety Act 2022 compliance provisions and golden thread requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: BSA 2022 compliance provisions demonstrate regulatory awareness and protect all parties.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: BSA compliance: gateway requirements, golden thread information management, and duty holder appointments.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: gateway compliance schedule, golden thread information format, and duty holder appointments documented.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Contractor's All Risks insurance not covering full reinstatement value of works
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Under-insurance means shortfall in recovery if works are damaged or destroyed.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: CAR insurance at full reinstatement value including materials, labour, and professional fees.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require CAR at full reinstatement value. Include: materials on/off-site, professional fees for redesign, and demolition costs.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No deadline for agreement of final account after practical completion
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Open-ended final account creates uncertainty and prevents financial close-out.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Final account submitted within 3-6 months of practical completion. Agreement within 12 months.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require final account submission within 6 months of practical completion. Agreement process with independent certification within 12 months.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: M&A transaction without comprehensive tax warranties and tax indemnity
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Undisclosed tax liabilities can fundamentally change deal economics post-completion.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Tax warranties: compliance with all tax obligations, no disputes, full provision for known liabilities. Specific tax indemnity.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require comprehensive tax warranties and specific tax deed covering: historical compliance, ongoing disputes, and transfer pricing.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Technology acquisition without comprehensive IP warranties
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: IP is often primary asset in tech acquisitions. Inadequate warranties create valuation risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: IP warranties: ownership, non-infringement, material licences, open source compliance, and no adverse claims.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require IP warranties: full ownership/valid licences, non-infringement, open source compliance, SBOM, and no pending/threatened claims.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: M&A warranty claims subject to reasonable de minimis and aggregate basket thresholds
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: De minimis and basket thresholds filter trivial claims while preserving meaningful protection.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: De minimis: 0.1% of purchase price. Basket: 0.5-1% of purchase price. Tipping vs deductible approach varies.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: de minimis at ~0.1% of price, basket at ~0.75%, and whether basket operates as tipping or deductible threshold.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: M&A warranty limitation period less than 18 months for general warranties
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Short limitation periods may prevent discovery of warranty breaches.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: General warranties: 18-24 months. Tax: 7 years. Title: perpetual. Environmental: 5-7 years.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: General warranties: 18 months minimum. Tax: statutory limitation period. Title and capacity: 6 years. Environmental: 5 years.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Shareholder agreement with clear pre-emption rights mechanism and fair value determination
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Well-structured pre-emption rights protect shareholders from unwanted third-party shareholders.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Pre-emption on transfer: pro-rata to existing shareholders, at fair market value determined by independent valuer.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: valuation mechanism (independent valuer), acceptance period (30-60 days), and partial acceptance provisions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI system making automated decisions with legal effects without human review option
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: GDPR Article 22 gives data subjects right not to be subject to solely automated decision-making with legal effects.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Human review mechanism for automated decisions with significant effects. Clear escalation path.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable where GDPR applies. Include: human review option, clear escalation path, and right to contest automated decisions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service generating content without content filtering or safety guardrails
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unfiltered AI content may produce harmful, defamatory, or legally problematic outputs.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Content safety filters, prompt injection protection, and harmful content detection.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: content safety filters, prompt injection protection, harmful content detection, and clear terms on prohibited use cases.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service includes comprehensive model governance: versioning, testing, audit trail, and rollback
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive model governance provides transparency and accountability for AI decisions.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Leading practice. Version control, A/B testing, performance monitoring, and rapid rollback capability.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: version history maintained, performance monitoring includes drift detection, and rollback can execute within defined SLA.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI/cloud service with comprehensive exit plan including data migration, model portability, and transition assistance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive exit planning prevents AI/cloud lock-in and ensures business continuity.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 12-month exit assistance, standard format data export, model portability, and configuration documentation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: data export formats are standard, model portability tested, transition timeline realistic, and assistance at reasonable rates.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service processing EU data without specified data residency or with US-only processing
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: EU data processed in non-adequate jurisdiction without safeguards violates GDPR.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: EU data residency for EU customers. Specify data centre locations and failover arrangements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require EU data residency: processing and storage within EEA. If US processing needed, require SCCs and TIA.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI platform without customer right to conduct security testing or vulnerability assessment
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without testing rights, customer cannot verify security posture of AI platform handling sensitive data.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual penetration test rights or shared results from provider's annual pen test. Vulnerability disclosure process.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: annual pen test results shared, or customer right to conduct testing with 30-day notice. Vulnerability disclosure process.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Foundation model licence with restrictive use limitations that conflict with intended business use
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Foundation model licences often contain use restrictions that may conflict with commercial deployment.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Commercial licence with: defined permitted uses, field of use, and clear restriction on competing model training.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Review licence restrictions against intended use cases. Ensure: commercial use permitted, output use unrestricted, and no competing model training.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Manufacturing/procurement contract without circular economy or waste reduction provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Circular Economy Action Plan and sector regulations increasingly require waste reduction commitments.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Waste reduction targets, take-back obligations, and recyclability requirements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include circular economy provisions: waste reduction targets, recyclability requirements, and take-back obligations for end-of-life products.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Supply chain contract with comprehensive ILO-aligned labour standards and monitoring
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: ILO-aligned labour standards demonstrate social responsibility and reduce supply chain risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: ILO core conventions: freedom of association, no forced labour, no child labour, non-discrimination. Regular audit.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: audit cadence (annual minimum), remediation process, worker grievance mechanism, and living wage commitment.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Agricultural or commodities supply chain without deforestation-free commitment
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) requires due diligence for deforestation-free supply chains.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Deforestation-free commitment with: GPS coordinates, due diligence documentation, and supply chain traceability.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include EUDR-aligned provisions: deforestation-free commitment, GPS traceability, due diligence documentation, and annual verification.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract fully compliant with public procurement regulations with transparent evaluation
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Full procurement compliance reduces challenge risk and ensures fair competition.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Compliance with applicable procurement directive. Transparent evaluation criteria. Standstill period observed.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: evaluation criteria documented, standstill period observed, and award decision properly reasoned.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Large government contract without SME subcontracting or supply chain diversification targets
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Government procurement policies increasingly require SME participation in supply chains.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: SME subcontracting targets: 25-33% of contract value. Regular reporting on SME spend.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept SME targets if funded. Include: SME subcontracting plan, reporting requirements, and measurement methodology.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government performance regime with excessive deductions that make contract uneconomic
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Punitive performance regimes deter quality providers and create perverse incentives.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Performance deductions capped at 15-20% of monthly fee. Balanced with earn-back mechanism.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Cap total deductions at 15% of monthly fee. Include earn-back mechanism for improved performance. Termination trigger at cap for 3 months.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract with funded innovation clause including shared IP and improvement mechanisms
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Innovation clauses ensure service evolution and demonstrate value for money over long terms.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Innovation fund (1-2% of contract value), shared IP for innovations, and annual innovation plan.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: innovation fund adequate, IP sharing mechanism clear, and innovation plan jointly developed.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract with sovereign immunity clause preventing enforcement of contractual rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Sovereign immunity prevents enforcement of judgments/awards against government entities.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Waiver of sovereign immunity for commercial contract enforcement. Subject to applicable law limitations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require waiver of sovereign immunity for: enforcement of judgments/awards, attachment of assets, and execution of remedies.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Transition services priced at significant premium above normal rates without justification
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Excessive transition pricing creates financial disincentive to exit, reinforcing lock-in.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Transition services at prevailing contract rates or cost-plus reasonable margin.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Transition services at prevailing rates. If premium justified (overtime, additional resources), cap at 125% of standard rates.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Cost-plus contract without cost verification or audit rights over cost basis
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without cost verification, pass-through costs cannot be validated as reasonable and actual.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Quarterly cost reports, annual audit rights, and clear definition of allowable costs.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: cost reporting (quarterly), audit rights over cost basis (annual), definition of allowable costs, and disallowed cost categories.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liquidated damages at proportionate rate with clear link to genuine pre-estimate of loss
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Proportionate liquidated damages provide certainty for both parties and are enforceable.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: LD rate: 0.5-1% per week of affected scope value, capped at 10-15%. Documented basis for calculation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: calculation basis documented, cap reasonable (10-15%), and adjustment mechanism for concurrent delay.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Annual price adjustment mechanism linked to specific CPI index with cap and application process
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: CPI-linked adjustment provides fair inflation protection with predictable budget impact.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual CPI adjustment: specific index defined, cap at CPI or 5% (whichever lower), and 90-day notice.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: specific CPI index reference, cap percentage, application date, and notice period for adjustment.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Service credit regime with earn-back mechanism for improved performance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Earn-back incentivises sustained improvement rather than just penalising failure.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Earn-back of up to 50% of deducted credits for exceeding targets in subsequent period.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: earn-back percentage reasonable, measurement period defined, and cannot exceed originally deducted credits.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International contract executed in two languages without specifying which prevails
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without prevailing language, translation disputes create interpretation uncertainty.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Specify prevailing language. If dual language, one prevails for interpretation purposes.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Specify one language as prevailing for interpretation. If dual language needed, include: prevailing language clause and qualified translator.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Technology transfer agreement without local content or technology localisation requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Many jurisdictions require technology localisation or local content in technology transfers.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Technology transfer with: local content plan, knowledge transfer obligations, and localisation timeline.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Address local content requirements: training obligations, knowledge transfer plan, and compliance with local technology transfer regulations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Choice of court agreement without considering enforceability of judgments in counterparty jurisdiction
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Court judgment may not be enforceable in counterparty's jurisdiction without treaty or convention.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Consider enforceability before choosing forum. Hague Convention on Choice of Court Agreements where applicable.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Verify judgment enforceability in counterparty jurisdiction. Consider arbitration (New York Convention) for universal enforceability.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: BAA termination provisions do not address PHI return or destruction
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: HIPAA requires return or destruction of PHI on termination. Absence is non-compliant.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: PHI return or destruction within 30 days. Certification of destruction. Exception for legally required retention.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: PHI return or destruction within 30 days of termination, certification of destruction, and legally required retention carve-out.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA without requirement for processor to maintain records of processing activities
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: GDPR Article 30(2) requires processors to maintain records. Absence is non-compliant.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Processor maintains records per Article 30(2). Made available to controller and supervisory authority on request.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require processor to maintain Article 30(2) records. Available to controller on request and to supervisory authority on demand.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA without requirement for processor personnel to be bound by confidentiality
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: GDPR Article 28(3)(b) requires processor to ensure persons authorised to process are under confidentiality.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: All processor personnel handling personal data bound by contractual or statutory confidentiality obligation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require all personnel processing personal data to be bound by written confidentiality obligation or statutory duty.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Data breach notification framework with clear timelines, content requirements, and cooperation obligations
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive breach notification framework meets regulatory requirements across jurisdictions.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 24-48 hour notification with: nature of breach, data affected, likely consequences, and mitigation measures.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: notification timeline meets strictest applicable regulation, content is comprehensive, and cooperation obligations clear.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SaaS contract with less than 30 days post-termination data access period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Short post-termination access makes data migration practically impossible for complex deployments.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 90-day post-termination data access period. Data in standard export formats.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require minimum 90-day post-termination data access. Standard export formats. Provider assistance at reasonable rates.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SaaS SLA with automatic service credit application based on monitoring data
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Automatic credits eliminate administrative burden and ensure credits are actually received.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Automatic credit application is best practice. Manual claim within 30-60 days as minimum.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: monitoring data accessible to customer, credit calculation transparent, and credits visible on invoice.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SaaS provider can unilaterally remove features or functionality without notice or consent
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unilateral feature removal may break customer workflows and violate the service commitment.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 90-day notice for feature deprecation. No removal of core features during subscription term.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Prohibit removal of core features during subscription term. 90-day notice for non-core changes. Alternative functionality provided.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liability exclusion for losses arising from events outside party's reasonable control embedded in liability clause
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Broad exclusion within liability clause effectively creates unlimited liability exclusion beyond force majeure.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Force majeure and liability exclusions kept separate. FM does not affect liability for pre-existing obligations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Remove from liability clause. Address force majeure exclusively in dedicated FM clause with proper notice, mitigation, and termination rights.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Aggregate liability cap resetting annually on contract anniversary with per-claim and aggregate limits
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Annual reset ensures liability protection keeps pace with ongoing contract value.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Aggregate cap at 12 months fees, resetting annually. Per-claim cap at 50% of aggregate.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: reset mechanism clear, per-claim and aggregate both defined, and carve-outs subject to super-cap.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Customer indemnification obligations extending beyond misuse to cover any claim arising from customer data
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Broad customer indemnity shifts provider's data security liability back to customer.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Customer indemnity limited to: misuse of service, breach of acceptable use policy, and customer content infringement.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit customer indemnity to: wilful misuse, breach of acceptable use, and IP infringement by customer content. Not for data security failures.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SaaS or data migration contract without warranty on accuracy of data import/migration
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Data corruption during migration can have significant operational and legal consequences.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Data migration warranty: validation testing, reconciliation reports, and correction period.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: data migration validation testing, reconciliation report, and 30-day correction period for any migration errors.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Termination fees on declining scale reducing over contract term with cap
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Declining termination fees balance provider investment recovery with customer flexibility.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Year 1: 50-75%. Year 2: 25-50%. Year 3+: 0-25%. Capped at actual stranded costs.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: declining schedule is reasonable, cap at actual stranded costs, and no fees on termination for cause.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Termination with structured wind-down period including knowledge transfer, data migration, and parallel run
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Well-structured wind-down ensures orderly transition without service disruption.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 90-day minimum wind-down: knowledge transfer, data migration, parallel operations, and documentation delivery.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: wind-down timeline realistic, resources committed, and service maintained at contracted levels during transition.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: CPI-linked price adjustment with cap but no floor, meaning prices can decrease
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: CPI with no floor means prices decrease in deflationary periods, potentially below cost.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: CPI with cap (3-5%) and floor (0%). No negative adjustments.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept cap. Add floor at 0% (no negative adjustments). If floor unacceptable, cap at 3% with 0% floor.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Technical disputes resolved by expert determination with binding effect
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Expert determination provides fast, cost-effective resolution for technical matters.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Expert determination for: technical performance disputes, service level calculations, and completion criteria.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept for technical matters. Verify: expert selection mechanism, binding effect, and no appeal on matters of fact.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Force majeure suspends performance but customer must continue paying during FM event
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Paying for services not received during FM is unreasonable beyond a short initial period.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Payment suspended for affected services during FM. Fixed costs continue for 30 days then suspend.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Suspend variable charges immediately. Fixed charges continue for 30 days maximum. Full suspension if FM exceeds 30 days.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual ongoing compliance representation with notification obligation for material non-compliance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Ongoing compliance representation with notification provides continuous assurance.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Mutual representation of compliance with all applicable laws, ongoing throughout term, with notification of material non-compliance.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: scope covers all relevant laws (data protection, anti-bribery, export controls), notification within 5 business days.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Change of control definition does not cover indirect changes in ownership or control
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Narrow change of control definition allows indirect ownership changes to bypass consent requirements.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Change of control covers: direct and indirect changes, voting control, board control, and beneficial ownership above 50%.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Broaden definition to include: direct and indirect changes, voting rights, board composition, and beneficial ownership above 50%.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Audit rights do not extend to provider's sub-processors or critical sub-contractors
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Data security is only as strong as the weakest link. Sub-processors must be auditable.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Audit rights extend to material sub-processors. Provider ensures flow-down of audit obligations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Extend audit rights to material sub-processors. Provider to ensure flow-down. Accept SOC 2 reports from sub-processors as alternative.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual confidentiality with standard exceptions, marking requirements, and 5-year survival
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive mutual confidentiality with clear exceptions provides balanced protection.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard exceptions: public domain, independent development, prior knowledge, legally compelled disclosure.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: exceptions are genuine, marking requirements practical, and survival period adequate for information sensitivity.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: General liability insurance at market levels with annual certificate and change notification
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Adequate general liability insurance provides financial backstop for third-party claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: EUR 5M general liability. Annual certificate. 30-day notification of material changes or cancellation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: coverage limits appropriate to contract risk, certificate provided annually, and notification for material changes.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SLA with defined response AND resolution times by severity with escalation and credit mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Both response and resolution targets with escalation provide comprehensive service assurance.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: P1: 15min/4hr, P2: 1hr/8hr, P3: 4hr/5d, P4: 8hr/20d. Escalation at 50% of target.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: severity classification is objective, escalation matrix defined, and credits linked to resolution not just response.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Employer pension contribution at statutory minimum without enhancement for senior roles
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Statutory minimum pension may be insufficient for senior hires expecting enhanced benefits.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Statutory minimum: 3-5% employer contribution. Senior roles: 8-15% employer contribution.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate employer contribution above statutory minimum. Senior roles: 10%+ employer contribution. Define contribution structure clearly.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International employment requiring relocation without relocation package or repatriation provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Relocation without support creates financial hardship and may deter acceptance of international roles.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Relocation package: moving costs, temporary housing (3-6 months), tax equalisation, and repatriation clause.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: moving costs, 3-month temporary housing, immigration support, tax equalisation, and repatriation within 12 months of termination.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Sick leave limited to statutory sick pay without company sick pay scheme
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Statutory sick pay is minimal. Company sick pay expected for professional and senior roles.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Company sick pay: 3-6 months full pay, 3-6 months half pay. PHI for long-term absence.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate company sick pay: 3 months full pay, 3 months half pay minimum. Include PHI/income protection for long-term illness.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Background check requirements proportionate to role with clear scope, consent, and GDPR compliance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Proportionate background checks protect employer while respecting employee privacy rights.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Checks proportionate to role: identity, right to work, qualifications, DBS for regulated roles. GDPR-compliant consent.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: checks proportionate to role, GDPR-compliant consent obtained, and clear data retention policy for results.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Loss and expense claims with no time-bar for notification or substantiation requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Open-ended claims without notification requirements create unquantifiable liability exposure.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Notification within 28 days of becoming aware. Substantiation within 42 days. Time-bar for late claims.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: 28-day notification requirement, 42-day substantiation deadline, and time-bar for claims not properly notified.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Construction supply chain without modern slavery statement or due diligence
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Construction sector is high-risk for modern slavery. Legal obligations apply under Modern Slavery Act.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Modern slavery statement, supply chain due diligence, right to audit, and immediate termination for violations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Include modern slavery clause: due diligence obligations, worker welfare standards, audit rights, and zero-tolerance termination.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Construction contract with BREEAM/LEED targets, carbon reporting, and sustainable materials requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Sustainability requirements in construction align with regulatory trends and market expectations.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: BREEAM Excellent or equivalent. Embodied carbon reporting. Sustainable material sourcing policy.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: certification target achievable, carbon measurement methodology, and material sourcing standards defined.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Parent company guarantee covering all subsidiary contractor obligations for duration plus defects period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: PCG provides financial backstop for contractor obligations including latent defects.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: PCG from parent with adequate financial standing. Duration: contract term plus defects liability period.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: parent company financial strength, PCG covers all obligations including defects, and duration includes run-off period.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: M&A transaction without employment warranties covering key employees and liabilities
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Undisclosed employment liabilities and key person departures can significantly impact deal value.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Employment warranties: key employees, material terms, pending claims, compliance, and pension obligations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require employment warranties: key employee list, material terms, pending/threatened claims, regulatory compliance, and pension funding status.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: M&A transaction without litigation warranties disclosing pending and threatened proceedings
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Undisclosed litigation can result in significant post-completion liabilities.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Litigation warranty: no pending or threatened proceedings, investigations, or regulatory actions.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require warranty: no pending, threatened, or anticipated proceedings. Full disclosure of all historical claims above de minimis threshold.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: M&A escrow with independent agent, defined release conditions, and appropriate retention period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Well-structured escrow provides security for warranty claims while ensuring timely release.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Escrow: 10-15% of purchase price, 18-24 month retention, release on warranty expiry minus notified claims.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: escrow percentage proportionate, retention aligns with warranty periods, and release mechanism clear.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Shareholder agreement with fair value put/call options and independent valuation mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Fair put/call options provide liquidity mechanism while preventing value extraction.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Put/call at fair market value determined by independent valuer. Exercise windows defined.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: valuation methodology defined, independent valuer selection mechanism, and exercise windows adequate.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International trade using documentary collection without bank payment guarantee
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Documentary collection relies on buyer's good faith. Less secure than letter of credit.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: D/P (documents against payment) preferred over D/A (documents against acceptance). LC for high-value shipments.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: For high-value transactions: upgrade to LC. If D/C acceptable: use D/P not D/A, and require credit insurance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Technology or goods export without dual-use classification assessment
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Dual-use goods require export licences. Failure to classify creates criminal liability risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: ECCN classification, licence determination, end-use certificates, and catch-all awareness.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Conduct ECCN classification. Determine licence requirements. Obtain end-use certificates. Implement catch-all screening.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International contract in jurisdiction with local content requirements without compliance plan
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Many jurisdictions require minimum local content in government and infrastructure contracts.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Local content plan: percentage targets, local employment, local procurement, and technology transfer.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include local content compliance plan: targets, measurement methodology, reporting, and penalties for non-compliance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International contract with ICC mediation as first step in dispute resolution with time-boxed process
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Mediation can resolve disputes faster and cheaper than arbitration while preserving relationships.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: ICC mediation: 60-day time limit, confidential, no prejudice to formal proceedings.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: time limit reasonable (60 days), confidentiality provisions, and formal proceedings preserved if mediation fails.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SCC TOMs annex with generic security measures not specific to the processing activities
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Generic TOMs fail to demonstrate that measures are appropriate to the specific processing risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: TOMs annex tailored to: data categories processed, volume, and risk level of processing.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require TOMs annex tailored to specific processing: encryption standards, access controls, monitoring, and testing appropriate to risk.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SCCs executed without completed sub-processor annex identifying all sub-processors
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Sub-processor annex is mandatory under SCCs. Missing annex invalidates the transfer mechanism.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Complete sub-processor annex: name, address, processing activities, and data categories for each sub-processor.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Complete sub-processor annex with: name, address, description of processing, and data categories for each sub-processor.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Contract includes CSRD-aligned ESG reporting obligations with defined metrics and frequency
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: CSRD-aligned reporting ensures regulatory compliance and enables corporate sustainability reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: CSRD-aligned: ESRS metrics, annual reporting, third-party assurance, and data format specified.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: metrics align with ESRS, reporting frequency adequate, assurance level specified, and data format compatible.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Manufacturing contract in water-stressed region without water stewardship provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Water scarcity creates operational and reputational risk. Regulatory requirements increasing.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Water usage monitoring, efficiency targets, and compliance with local water regulations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include water stewardship provisions: usage monitoring, efficiency targets (reduction per unit), and compliance with local water regulations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Large-scale transformation contract without just transition provisions for affected workers
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Just transition provisions protect against social impact claims and align with EU Taxonomy requirements.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Reskilling programmes, redeployment support, and social impact assessment.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include just transition provisions: reskilling commitment, redeployment support, and social impact assessment for affected communities.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Advisory contract with government without ethical walls for conflicting engagements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without ethical walls, advisor may inadvertently disclose confidential information from competing engagements.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Information barriers: separate teams, restricted access, compliance monitoring, and disclosure obligation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Implement ethical walls: separate teams, restricted document access, compliance officer oversight, and quarterly certification.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract with comprehensive transparency obligations including pricing and performance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Transparency obligations build public trust and meet accountability requirements.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Open book pricing, performance reporting, and FOI compliance with commercial sensitivity protection.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: commercial sensitivity protections adequate, reporting format specified, and publication schedule defined.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract potentially constituting state aid without subsidy control assessment
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Subsidy Control Act 2022 (UK) and EU State Aid rules require assessment. Non-compliance risks clawback.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Subsidy control assessment before contract execution. Market economy operator test where applicable.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Ensure subsidy control assessment completed. Include: compliance representation, notification obligations, and clawback mechanism.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Service credit mechanism with tiered credits, earn-back, and termination trigger for persistent failure
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive service credit mechanism incentivises performance and provides meaningful remedies.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Tiered credits (5-30% of monthly fee), earn-back at 50%, and termination right if credits exceed cap for 3 months.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: credit tiers proportionate to impact, earn-back mechanism fair, and termination trigger clearly defined.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Step-in rights with defined triggers, process, cost allocation, and restoration mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Well-defined step-in rights provide emergency continuity mechanism without ambiguity.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Step-in triggered by: persistent material failure, insolvency event. Customer bears costs during step-in.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: triggers clearly defined, process includes notification, cost allocation fair, and restoration criteria objective.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Data breach super-cap at 2-5x annual fees with comprehensive carve-out coverage
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Adequate super-cap for data breach provides meaningful protection while managing provider risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Super-cap at 2-5x annual fees for: data breach, IP infringement, and confidentiality breach.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: super-cap covers all data-related claims, notification costs included, and regulatory fines covered where legally permissible.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: IP indemnification with defend-and-hold-harmless, modify/replace option, and refund as last resort
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive IP indemnification provides full protection against third-party IP claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Provider: defend, hold harmless, modify/replace to non-infringing, refund if unable. Customer: misuse indemnity.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: covers all deliverables and services, modify/replace at provider cost, and refund mechanism clear.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Provider indemnification covering IP infringement, data breach, and confidentiality breach with clear process
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Well-scoped provider indemnification allocates risk to the party best able to manage it.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Provider indemnifies for: IP infringement, data breach, and confidentiality breach. Standard procedure with mitigation duties.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: scope covers all key risk areas, procedure includes notification/cooperation/mitigation, and subject to super-cap.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Net-60 payment terms with clear invoicing process, late payment interest, and dispute mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Net-60 with full process provides acceptable balance for larger enterprises.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Net-60 acceptable for large enterprises. Interest on late payment. Dispute process for contested amounts.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept for large enterprise. Verify: invoice requirements clear, interest rate specified, and dispute process prevents suspension.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual non-solicitation of directly involved employees for 12 months with anti-raiding focus
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Reasonable non-solicitation protects staffing investment while allowing general recruitment.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 12-month mutual non-solicitation of directly involved personnel. General recruitment advertising excluded.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: applies to active solicitation only, general recruitment excluded, and scope limited to project personnel.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Services warranty of reasonable skill and care consistent with industry best practice
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Industry-standard services warranty provides meaningful quality commitment.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Reasonable skill and care consistent with best industry practice. Qualified personnel. Re-performance remedy.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: re-performance obligation for defective services, timeframe for re-performance, and escalation if repeated failure.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Bespoke work product owned by customer with provider retaining licence to general tools and methodologies
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Clear work product ownership with provider tool licence is market-standard allocation.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Customer owns bespoke deliverables. Provider retains background IP and general tools with licence to customer.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: work product clearly defined, licence to general tools is non-exclusive and perpetual, and boundary is clear.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Confidentiality clause with return/destruction obligation on termination and written certification
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Clear return/destruction obligation ensures confidential information does not persist beyond need.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Return or destruction within 30 days, written certification, exception for legally required retention and archival copies.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: 30-day timeframe, certification mechanism, legally required retention carve-out, and archival copy exception reasonable.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Governing law and dispute resolution venue are aligned and in mutually convenient jurisdiction
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Aligned governing law and venue eliminates jurisdictional complexity and reduces costs.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Governing law matches dispute resolution venue. Both in jurisdiction with established commercial law.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: venue has established commercial court or arbitration centre, and judgments enforceable in both parties' jurisdictions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Employment contract without reference to disciplinary procedure or natural justice protections
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without disciplinary process, termination may be deemed unfair regardless of underlying reason.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Reference to disciplinary procedure: investigation, hearing, right to be accompanied, and appeal.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include reference to disciplinary procedure meeting ACAS Code of Practice or equivalent. Ensure natural justice protections.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Employment contract references comprehensive grievance procedure with clear escalation and timelines
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Clear grievance procedure demonstrates good employment practice and reduces tribunal risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Informal resolution, formal written complaint, investigation, hearing, and appeal. Timelines at each stage.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: procedure meets ACAS Code, timelines reasonable, right to be accompanied, and appeal to senior management.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Employment contract offers enhanced parental leave above statutory minimum with return guarantee
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Enhanced parental leave is a competitive benefit that aids retention and demonstrates good practice.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Enhanced: 16-26 weeks full pay (maternity), 4-6 weeks full pay (paternity). KIT days. Return guarantee.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: enhanced terms clearly defined, KIT day provisions, return to same or equivalent role, and flexible return options.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Performance bond at 10% of contract value from rated surety with clear call conditions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Performance bond provides employer with financial security for contractor performance failure.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 10% of contract value. On-demand or conditional. From rated surety. Duration: contract plus defects.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: bond type (on-demand vs conditional), surety rating, call conditions clear, and duration covers defects period.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Advance payment to contractor without advance payment guarantee or bond
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unsecured advance payment creates credit risk if contractor becomes insolvent.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Advance payment guarantee from rated bank. Reduces pro-rata as work progresses.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require advance payment guarantee: from rated bank, reducing as work progresses, and repayable in full on termination.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: M&A without warranty that target has no material contracts that could be terminated on change of control
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Change of control termination rights in target's contracts could destroy acquired value.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Warranty: no material contract with change of control termination. Disclosure of all change of control provisions.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require warranty disclosing all material contracts with change of control provisions. Obtain key customer/supplier consents pre-completion.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: M&A warranties include comprehensive regulatory compliance covering all material regulations
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive compliance warranties protect buyer from undisclosed regulatory exposure.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Compliance warranty: all material laws and regulations, no investigations, and adequate compliance programme.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: scope covers sector-specific regulations, data protection, anti-bribery, sanctions, and environmental compliance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Price benchmarking rights with defined methodology, qualified benchmarker, and adjustment mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Structured benchmarking ensures pricing remains fair over long contract terms.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual benchmarking right from year 3. Independent benchmarker. Adjustment if pricing exceeds market by >10%.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: benchmarking methodology defined, benchmarker independence required, and adjustment mechanism bilateral.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Government contract with clear conflict of interest provisions, disclosure obligations, and management plan
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive conflict management protects integrity of government engagement.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Disclosure of all potential conflicts, management plan, ethical walls, and ongoing monitoring.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: disclosure obligations comprehensive, management plan practical, and monitoring mechanism effective.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Industrial or construction contract without environmental remediation provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Environmental contamination liability can exceed contract value. Allocation must be clear.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Baseline environmental assessment. Remediation responsibility allocated by cause. Insurance required.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: baseline environmental assessment, cause-based remediation allocation, environmental insurance, and notification obligations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Drug and alcohol testing policy for safety-critical roles with GDPR-compliant process
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Testing for safety-critical roles is legitimate when proportionate and GDPR-compliant.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Testing limited to safety-critical roles. Random and for-cause. GDPR-compliant processing. Clear consequences.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept for safety-critical roles. Verify: proportionate to role, GDPR lawful basis, clear process, and rehabilitation support.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Employer without equal pay audit or representation of pay equity compliance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: EU Pay Transparency Directive and national legislation increasingly require pay equity reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Pay equity audit, gender pay gap reporting, and representation of compliance with equal pay legislation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: equal pay representation, commitment to pay equity audits, and compliance with applicable pay transparency requirements.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service contract with right to conduct algorithmic audits and access to model documentation
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Algorithmic audit rights enable verification of AI fairness, accuracy, and compliance.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Annual algorithmic audit right, model card documentation, and bias testing results shared.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: audit scope covers fairness and accuracy, model documentation adequate, and audit results actionable.
